paper_focus.title: Influence of Al content and precipitation state on the mechanical behavior of austenitic high-Mn low-density steels
paper_focus.research_objective: To clarify the role of precipitation state in the strain hardening mechanisms of low-density Fe–Mn–Al–C steels.
main_process_variables: casting and hot-rolling: reheat 1200°C, hot-rolled 75% reduction at 1100°C, water quench
solution treatment: 1100°C for 2h under Ar, water quench
annealing treatments: short annealing 450°C/1h; long annealing 2Al: 650°C/72h, 8Al: 600°C/24h
tensile testing: room temperature, initial strain rate 5×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹
interrupted tensile tests to different strain levels for microstructure evolution
microscopy: ECCI and TEM on longitudinal sections (perpendicular to tensile axis)
expected_information_axis: mechanical properties (yield stress, ultimate tensile strength, uniform elongation, strain hardening stages)
deformation mechanisms (planar slip, twinning, shearing of κ-carbides)
